title: Observability & Session Audit
summary: One Observability page brings tokens & cost, sensitive data, tools, feedback, and sessions together across your agents (scoped to what you can access). The new Sessions audit drills in: expand a session to see every turn in the same columns, then expand a turn for its full reasoning, tool calls, result, and sensitivity — masked by default, admin-only reveal.

title: LLM Activity Tracing
summary: A step-by-step trace for every Slack thread — the agent's reasoning, tool calls, and final answer in order, with per-step tokens, cost, latency, and model.

title: Sensitive Data Monitoring
summary: Flagging of PII, secrets, and credential access in agent tool calls and replies — with severity tiers, an audit feed, and in-trace highlighting (masked by default, admin-only reveal). Set each agent to Off, Deterministic (rules), or Smart, which adds an LLM pass that catches obfuscated PII the rules miss ("caught by LLM") and takes per-agent guidance. Exfiltration flows trace a value from where it enters to where it leaves, and secrets can be optionally redacted from replies.

title: Custom Date Ranges & 6-Month Retention
summary: Filter Activity, Usage, Tools, and Sensitive views by a custom date range. Trace data is automatically pruned to a rolling 6 months and idle session clones after 7 days.
